Job Title: Events Manager
Reports to: Chief Development & External Relations Officer
Professional Band: Managerial
Department: Development & External Relations
Job Classification: Full-Time (FT), exempt 
JOB DESCRIPTION
The Events Manager is responsible for planning, coordinating, and executing high-impact fundraising and community engagement events that advance the AIM Center’s mission and strengthen relationships with donors, sponsors, and community partners. This position plays a key role in delivering exceptional event experiences—from early planning and sponsor engagement to logistics, stewardship, and post-event follow-up. 
The successful candidate is a self-starter and will bring strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and a proactive approach to managing complex event logistics. They will work closely with the Chief Development & External Relations Officer and the development team to ensure AIM Center’s signature events—such as the Strike Out the Stigma Bowling Tournament and the Mental Health Hero Awards Annual Fundraising Dinner & Auction —are well-executed, mission-centered, and financially successful. 
This role requires a collaborative professional who thrives in a fast-paced environment, communicates effectively with diverse stakeholders, and takes pride in creating meaningful opportunities for the community to support AIM Center’s work. The Events Manager will uphold the highest ethical standards of the profession and be enthusiastic about AIM’s mission of working side by side and creating overwhelming opportunity for and with individuals with serious mental illness. 
SALARY AND SCHEDULE
Salary: $45,000 – $50,000 per year
Schedule: Full-time, exempt 
Working hours are 8 AM to 4 PM Monday to Friday with occasional evenings and weekends required to support fundraising events, donor engagement opportunities, and community outreach activities. 
ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES INCLUDE, BUT ARE NOT LIMITED TO: 
Event Strategy & Fundraising Growth
Develop and implement a comprehensive annual event strategy that aligns with the AIM Center’s fundraising and community engagement goals.
Establish revenue targets, sponsorship benchmarks, and attendance goals for signature fundraising events and campaigns.
Identify opportunities to expand and strengthen event-based fundraising through new sponsorship opportunities, corporate engagement strategies, and innovative donor experiences.
Evaluate event performance using financial outcomes, participation data, and stakeholder feedback to continuously improve future events.
Collaborate with the development team to integrate events into broader fundraising initiatives including corporate partnerships, donor stewardship, and community engagement strategies.  
Event Planning & Execution
Lead the planning, coordination and execution of AIM Center’s fundraising and community events, including logistics, timelines, vendor coordination, registration systems, and on-site management.
Plan and implement signature fundraising events such as Strike Out the Stigma Bowling Tournament, Mental Health Heroes Awards Fundraising Dinner and Auction, Art Showcases, and other mission-centered engagement opportunities.
Develop detailed project plans, run-of-show documents, internal communications, and event timelines to ensure smooth and professional event execution.
Coordinate event logistics including venue setup, catering, audiovisual needs, signage, volunteer assignments, guest experience, and accessibility considerations.
Ensure all events reflect the welcoming, mission-driven culture of the AIM Center. 
Event Operation & Systems Management
Develop and maintain standardized event planning systems including timelines, templates, vendor databases, sponsorship tracking tools, and operational workflows.
Maintain organized documentation of event plans, vendor contacts, sponsorship records, and operational procedures.
Use CRM and event management tools to track donor engagement, sponsorship participation, and event outcomes. 
Sponsorship & Donor Engagement
Support corporate sponsorship strategy and stewardship efforts in partnership with the Chief Development & External Affairs Officer.
Prepare sponsorship materials and coordinate sponsor benefit fulfillment including recognition, event participation, and post-event stewardship efforts.
Maintain strong relationships with corporate partners, vendors and community supports to strengthen long-term engagement with AIM Center events.
Oversee accurate documentation and tracking of sponsorship commitments, event contributions, and donor engagement activities within the CRM system.
Support donor recognition efforts through thoughtful event experiences, signage, and follow-up communications.  
Silent Auction & Fundraising Logistics
Coordinate silent auction procurement efforts, including potential donors, managing outreach, tracking item commitments, and organizing item descriptions and displays.
Manage silent auction logistics including packaging, bid sheets or digital auction platforms and guest engagement during events.
Ensure auction donors receive appropriate recognition and stewardship following events.
Monitor auction performance and perform and provide recommendations for improving fundraising outcomes.
Budget Management & Oversight
Manage event budgets to ensure responsible stewardship of organizational resources and strong return on investment.
Track event revenue and expenses including sponsorships, ticket sales, auctions, and vendor costs.
Monitor financial performance of events and prepare reports summarizing outcomes, trends and growth opportunities.
Negotiate vendor contracts and service agreements to maximize value while maintaining high-quality event experiences. 
Community Outreach & Tabling Engagement
Support AIM Center’s participation in community outreach events, tabling opportunities and public engagement activities that increase awareness of the organization’s mission and programs.
Work in close partnership with the Community Outreach Director to identify, evaluate and coordinate strategic tabling opportunities that connect AIM Center with community members, partners, and potential supporters.
Develop systems and schedules to ensure consistent representation of AIM Center at relevant community events, health fairs, nonprofit expos, and local engagement opportunities.
Coordinate logistics for outreach events including registration, materials, staffing delegation, transportation of supplies, and on-site setup in conjunction with other team members as appropriate.
Track participation and outcomes of community engagement activities and provide recommendations to leadership for strengthening visibility and partnerships.
Support staff and member participation in outreach events to ensure experiences reflect the welcoming and inclusive culture of the Clubhouse model. 
Leadership & Volunteer Coordination
Recruit, train and coordinate event-day volunteers, committees and community supporters involved in fundraising events as needed and as appropriate.
Provide leadership and direction to staff, volunteers and event committees to ensure alignment with event goals and organizational standards.
Foster strong relationships with community partners, sponsors, and vendors to build long-term engagement with AIM Center events.
Serve as the primary internal coordinator for event planning efforts, ensuring strong collaboration and communication among staff teams. 
Clubhouse Generalist Duties
Support a safe, inclusive, and culturally responsive Clubhouse environment.
Advocate for member rights and meaningful participation in operations.
Support fidelity to Clubhouse International Standards and AIM Center policies.
Encourage member participation in employment programs (TE, SE, IE).
Serve as Transitional Employment Job Coach/Placement Manager as assigned.
Transport Clubhouse members as needed (training provided).
Participate in staff rotation coverage, socials, employment dinners, holidays, and special events.
Collaborate with members on social and recreational activities.
Participate in continuous quality improvement and professional development.
Other duties as assigned. 
EDUCATION, JOB QUALIFICATIONS AND SKILLS REQUIRED
Bachelor’s Degree in Event Management, Nonprofit Management, Communications, Hospitality, Marketing, or a related field preferred.
Demonstrated experience coordinating events, fundraising initiatives, or community engagement activities. Two years of demonstrated experience preferred.
Strong project management skills with the ability to coordinate multiple timelines, vendors, and moving pieces simultaneously.
Excellent organizational skills and meticulous attention to detail.
Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to build relationships with donors, sponsors, vendors, and volunteers.
Ability to communicate clearly and professionally in both written and verbal formats.
Experience with donor databases, event management platforms, or CRM systems preferred. Neon CRM highly preferred.
Ability to work independently while contributing collaboratively to a small, high-performing team.
Ability to approach tasks and challenges with strategic thinking, structured methodologies, and a commitment to continuous improvement.
Flexibility to support evening and weekend events as required.
Passion for community impact and enthusiasm for AIM Center’s mission of working side by side with individuals living with serious mental illness.
Must pass a pre-employment background check.
Valid Tennessee driver’s license with the ability to obtain F-Endorsement within 14 days and a three-year clean Motor Vehicle Record and no DUI/DWI.
Reliable transportation and proof of automobile liability insurance.
First Aid and CPR certification (or willingness to obtain).
Successful completion of required Relias training modules.

The Director of Philanthropy plays an integral role in creating and driving the strategy for the philanthropy team, ensuring that donors—in all parts of the donor pipeline—are properly cultivated, solicited, and stewarded through effective moves management and operational excellence.
Essential Duties & Responsibilities
1.	Develop and implement fundraising campaigns to meet the organization’s current and future needs, promoting the organization’s shift to proactive growth of annual philanthropic supporters who want to invest in the Tennessee Aquarium.
2.	Oversee the moves management processes and procedures in the CRM database and ticketing systems, ensuring all donors receive accurate and timely communication and engagement with Tennessee Aquarium. Collaborate with the Development Support Specialist to ensure these processes are executed efficiently, with a critical eye for detail.
3.	Inform data-driven campaign decisions by regularly analyzing reports and performance metrics, providing feedback to the philanthropy team, and adjusting fundraising goals as needed.
4.	Work with the philanthropy team through group and one-on-one meetings, providing consistent communication that ensures that all fundraising strategies and targeted campaigns are executed effectively and timely to meet or exceed department goals.
5.	Identify and research current giving trends and best practices and work to incorporate them into department fundraising strategies.
6.	Ensure effective and efficient renewal and growth of the annual fund-giving circle supporters through management of the Development Manager.
7.	Ensure effective and efficient growth of major gifts and the planned giving society through management of the Strategic Giving Officer.
8.	Oversee donor stewardship through effective communication and event efforts through the management of the Fundraising Stewardship Specialist, hiring Summer 2026.
9.	Work collaboratively with the VP, Chief Philanthropy Officer to establish overall department goals and priorities, provide strong leadership to the team, and create and oversee departmental budgets and other high-level projects as needed.
10.	Work with the VP, Chief Philanthropy Officer, to develop and maintain a personal portfolio of donors within the next 6-12 months.  
Knowledge, Skills & Abilities Required
•	Excellent  communication skills (verbal, written, listening).
•	Exhibits strong interpersonal skills, emotional intelligence, and professional attitude in the workplace.
•	Strong organizational and project management skills with a proven track record of managing competing priorities efficiently while ensuring accuracy and exceptional attention to detail.
•	Ability to lead, inspire, and motivate a team.
•	Excellent relationship management skills, including bringing stakeholders closer to the organization’s mission and vision.
•	Strong computer proficiency and accuracy, with the ability to learn new systems quickly.
•	Must uphold strict confidentiality in all aspects of the role
Education & Experience Required
•	College degree in a related field required (Non-Profit Management, Public Administration, Communications/Public Relations, Business Administration/Management, Fundraising, etc.)
•	7-10 years of progressively responsible fundraising experience and a proven record of successful cultivation and solicitation of gifts.
•	3-5 years’ experience working with donor CRM systems required.
•	Demonstrated working knowledge of all areas of fundraising, with a particular emphasis on managing donor pipeline and developing new prospects.
•	Proven record of setting and achieving both short- and long-term goals and implementing performance evaluation strategies.
Supervises
•	Strategic Giving Officer
•	Development Manager
•	Development Support Specialist
•	Fundraising Stewardship Specialist
Working  Conditions
•	Typical office environment
•	Occasional work in event/public environments
•	Occasional  evening and weekend work
•	Exposure to a variety of environments within the aquarium, including public exhibit spaces, behind-the-scenes areas, and outdoor settings
Physical Demands
The physical demands described below are representative of those that may be encountered while performing the essential functions of this position. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.
•	Extended  periods of keyboarding and computer use
•	Extended periods of remaining in a stationary position
•	Occasional movement throughout the facility and event spaces, including navigating stairs or large venues
•	Regular communication with internal and external personnel in person, by phone, and electronically
•	Occasional movement or positioning of event-related items weighing up to 20 pounds
Full-time, salaried; Typical Monday – Friday office hours, will be required to work nights/weekends/events periodically as needed.
To apply, submit an online application, cover letter, resume, and references through the Aquarium’s career site at https://bit.ly/TNAQUA-careers
All submissions must be received by June 11, 2026, at 5:00 PM.
